primary_meaning: ichthyology, the scientific study of fish
secondary_meaning: the branch of zoology that deals with fish classification, behavior, and evolution
usage_notes: Use with 'l'ichthyologie moderne', 'étude ichthyologique', 'spécialiste en ichthyologie'
common_collocations: ichthyologie marine, ichthyologie d'eau douce, étude ichthyologique, recherche ichthyologique
image_search_query: fish species marine research taxonomy study
image_context: Laboratory researcher studying fish specimens and species classification
example_1: L'ichthyologie nous aide à comprendre la biodiversité remarquable des écosystèmes aquatiques.
example_1_translation: Ichthyology helps us understand the remarkable biodiversity of aquatic ecosystems.
example_2: Les avancées en ichthyologie ont permis de découvrir des espèces de poissons complètement nouvelles.
example_2_translation: Advances in ichthyology have led to the discovery of completely new fish species.
example_3: Les ichthyologues modernes utilisent des technologies génomiques pour classifier les poissons.
example_3_translation: Modern ichthyologists use genomic technologies to classify fish.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'ik-tee-oh-LOH-zhee'. Stress on 'LOH'. The 'y' sounds like 'i'.
memory_hook: From Greek 'ichthys' (fish) + 'logos' (study). The science of studying fish.
related_words: zoologie, poisson, espèce, biodiversité, écosystème, taxonomie
common_mistakes: Not confusing with 'ichtyo-' prefix - ichthyology is specifically the study discipline of fish.

primary_meaning: to splash or spray with water; to scatter foam or droplets
secondary_meaning: nautical term describing water splashing against ship's bow or rocks
common_collocations: jaspiller l'écume, jaspiller les gouttelettes, jaspiller sur les rochers
image_search_query: splashing water foam ocean waves ship bow
image_context: Ocean waves crashing against boat prow with spray and foam
example_1: La proue du navire jaspille l'écume au milieu de l'océan tempétueux.
example_1_translation: The ship's prow splashes foam in the middle of the tempestuous ocean.
example_2: Le courant violent fait jaspiller les gouttelettes sur les rochers côtiers.
example_2_translation: The strong current causes droplets to splash on coastal rocks.
example_3: Sous l'impact des vagues, l'eau jaspille haut dans le ciel marin.
example_3_translation: Under the impact of waves, water splashes high into the sea sky.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'zhah-spee-YAY'. Stress on 'YAY'. French 'j' sounds like 'zh' in 'measure'.
memory_hook: Onomatopoeia - mimics the sound of water splashing. Related to 'jaspe' (speckled).
related_words: écume, éclaboussure, mer, tempête, vague, projection
common_mistakes: Not confusing with 'gaspiller' (to waste) - jaspiller is specifically nautical water action.

primary_meaning: keratotomy, a surgical procedure involving incision of the cornea
secondary_meaning: historical eye surgery technique used to correct myopia before LASIK technology
usage_notes: Use with 'une kératotomie radiaire', 'intervention de kératotomie', 'technique kératotomique'
common_collocations: kératotomie radiaire, kératotomie astigmate, technique kératotomique, chirurgie réfractive
image_search_query: eye surgery cornea keratotomy incision procedure
image_context: Ophthalmological surgical procedure showing corneal incision technique
example_1: La kératotomie radiaire était une technique chirurgicale courante pour corriger la myopie avant l'invention du LASIK.
example_1_translation: Radial keratotomy was a common surgical technique to correct myopia before LASIK invention.
example_2: Les techniques modernes ont largement remplacé la kératotomie traditionnelle.
example_2_translation: Modern techniques have largely replaced traditional keratotomy.
example_3: La kératotomie a marqué une étape importante dans l'histoire de la chirurgie réfractive.
example_3_translation: Keratotomy marked an important stage in the history of refractive surgery.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'kay-rah-toh-toh-MEE'. Stress on 'MEE'. Greek-derived medical term.
memory_hook: From Greek 'keras' (horn/cornea) + 'tome' (cut). A cutting procedure on the cornea.
related_words: cornée, chirurgie, ophtalmologie, myopie, correction visuelle, LASIK
common_mistakes: Not confusing keratotomy (surgical cut) with 'kerathermie' (thermal treatment) or 'kératomètre' (measurement).

primary_meaning: lipase, an enzyme that catalyzes the breakdown of fats into fatty acids
secondary_meaning: serum enzyme measured in blood tests to diagnose pancreatic disorders
usage_notes: Use with 'la lipase pancréatique', 'la lipase gastrique', 'taux de lipase'
common_collocations: lipase pancréatique, lipase gastrique, taux de lipase, inhibiteur de lipase
image_search_query: lipase enzyme digestion fat breakdown molecular diagram
image_context: Molecular diagram showing lipase enzyme breaking down triglycerides into fatty acids
example_1: La lipase pancréatique joue un rôle crucial dans la digestion des lipides alimentaires.
example_1_translation: Pancreatic lipase plays a crucial role in digesting dietary lipids.
example_2: Les inhibiteurs de lipase sont utilisés dans le traitement médical de l'obésité.
example_2_translation: Lipase inhibitors are used in the medical treatment of obesity.
example_3: Un taux élevé de lipase sanguine peut indiquer une pancrédipatie.
example_3_translation: Elevated serum lipase levels can indicate pancreatic inflammation.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'lee-PAHZ'. Stress on 'PAHZ'. The 's' is pronounced as 'z' in French.
memory_hook: From Latin 'lipum' (fat) + Greek '-ase' (enzyme suffix). The fat-breaking enzyme.
related_words: enzyme, digestion, pancréas, lipide, graissse, triglycéride
common_mistakes: Not confusing 'lipase' with 'lactase' - lipase breaks down fats; lactase breaks down lactose.

primary_meaning: mitochondria, cellular organelle responsible for energy production through ATP synthesis
secondary_meaning: the powerhouse of the cell containing its own DNA and RNA
common_collocations: mitochondrie cellulaire, dysfonction mitochondriale, ADN mitochondrial, énergie mitochondriale
image_search_query: mitochondria cell organelle structure energy ATP production
image_context: Colorized electron microscopy image of mitochondria showing cristae and matrix
example_1: Les mitochondries sont les véritables centrales énergétiques de la cellule.
example_1_translation: Mitochondria are the true powerhouses of the cell.
example_2: La dysfonction mitochondriale est impliquée dans de nombreuses maladies génétiques héréditaires.
example_2_translation: Mitochondrial dysfunction is involved in many hereditary genetic diseases.
example_3: Chaque mitochondrie contient son propre ADN distinct de l'ADN nucléaire.
example_3_translation: Each mitochondrion contains its own DNA distinct from nuclear DNA.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'mee-toh-KOHN-dree'. Stress on 'KOHN'. The final 'e' is silent.
memory_hook: From Greek 'mitos' (thread) + 'chondrion' (granule). Thread-like granules in cells.
related_words: cellule, organite, énergie, ATP, biologie, ADN
common_mistakes: Singular 'mitochondrie' / plural 'mitochondries' in French; English often uses 'mitochondria' for both.

primary_meaning: neurotransmitter, a chemical messenger transmitting signals across synapses
secondary_meaning: molecular substance released by neurons that affects target cells
usage_notes: Use with 'un neurotransmetteur', 'les neurotransmetteurs', 'équilibre des neurotransmetteurs'
common_collocations: neurotransmetteur inhibiteur, neurotransmetteur excitateur, sérotonine, dopamine, GABA
image_search_query: neurotransmitter synapse nerve cell communication molecular
image_context: Synaptic junction showing neurotransmitter molecules crossing synaptic cleft
example_1: La sérotonine est un neurotransmetteur clé impliqué dans la régulation de l'humeur et du sommeil.
example_1_translation: Serotonin is a key neurotransmitter involved in mood and sleep regulation.
example_2: Les déséquilibres en neurotransmetteurs peuvent causer des troubles psychiques sérieux.
example_2_translation: Neurotransmitter imbalances can cause serious mental disorders.
example_3: De nombreux médicaments psychiatriques agissent en régulant les niveaux de neurotransmetteurs.
example_3_translation: Many psychiatric medications work by regulating neurotransmitter levels.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'noo-roh-trahn-smeh-TÖR'. Stress on final syllable. German-influenced pronunciation.
memory_hook: From neuro- (nerve) + transmetteur (transmitter). A substance that transmits nerve signals.
related_words: nerf, synapse, cerveau, dopamine, sérotonine, neurone, signal

primary_meaning: oscillation, the periodic motion of an object back and forth about an equilibrium position
secondary_meaning: regular or rhythmic variation or fluctuation in any periodic system
common_collocations: oscillation harmonique, oscillation amortie, oscillation économique, oscillation pendulaire
image_search_query: pendulum oscillation wave motion physics diagram
image_context: Pendulum swinging back and forth showing oscillatory motion pattern
example_1: L'oscillation régulière du pendule suit une trajectoire mathématiquement prévisible.
example_1_translation: The regular oscillation of the pendulum follows a mathematically predictable path.
example_2: Les oscillations économiques reflètent les cycles naturels de croissance et récession.
example_2_translation: Economic oscillations reflect the natural cycles of growth and recession.
example_3: L'amortissement progressif des oscillations dépend de la friction et de la résistance.
example_3_translation: The progressive damping of oscillations depends on friction and resistance.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'oss-ee-lah-SYOHN'. Stress on 'SYOHN'. The 'o' sounds like 'aw' in 'off'.
memory_hook: From Latin 'oscillare' (to swing). Rhythmic back-and-forth motion.
related_words: vibration, pendule, période, fréquence, harmonie, mouvement

primary_meaning: paleontology, the scientific study of fossilized remains and prehistoric life
secondary_meaning: the discipline that uses fossils to reconstruct evolutionary history and ancient environments
usage_notes: Use with 'la paléontologie moderne', 'paléontologie marine', 'découverte paléontologique'
common_collocations: paléontologie marine, paléontologie vertébrée, découverte paléontologique, chercheur paléontologue
image_search_query: paleontology fossil dig dinosaur skeleton excavation
image_context: Paleontologist carefully excavating fossilized dinosaur skeleton from rock
example_1: La paléontologie nous révèle l'évolution fascinante des espèces sur des millions d'années.
example_1_translation: Paleontology reveals the fascinating evolution of species over millions of years.
example_2: Les découvertes paléontologiques majeures remettent régulièrement en question les théories établies.
example_2_translation: Major paleontological discoveries regularly challenge established theories.
example_3: La paléontologie moléculaire permet maintenant d'extraire et d'analyser l'ADN ancien.
example_3_translation: Molecular paleontology now allows extraction and analysis of ancient DNA.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'pah-lay-ohn-toh-LOH-zhee'. Stress on 'LOH'. Six syllables total.
memory_hook: From Greek 'palaios' (ancient) + 'on' (being) + 'logos' (study). Study of ancient life.
related_words: fossile, dinosaure, évolution, géologie, archéologie, stratigraphie
common_mistakes: Not confusing 'paléontologie' (study of fossils) with 'archéologie' (study of human artifacts).

primary_meaning: quartz, a hard crystalline mineral composed of silicon dioxide (SiO2)
secondary_meaning: the most abundant mineral in Earth's crust; used in watches, glass, and electronics
usage_notes: Use with 'du quartz', 'cristal de quartz', 'quartz rose'
common_collocations: cristal de quartz, montre à quartz, quartz rose, quartz fumé, gisement de quartz
image_search_query: quartz crystal mineral transparent geology specimen
image_context: Clear quartz crystal cluster on geological specimen display
example_1: Le quartz est l'un des minéraux les plus abondants de la croûte terrestre.
example_1_translation: Quartz is one of the most abundant minerals in the Earth's crust.
example_2: Les montres à quartz offrent une précision temporelle remarquable et une grande fiabilité.
example_2_translation: Quartz watches offer remarkable time precision and great reliability.
example_3: Le quartz rose est apprécié des collectionneurs pour ses propriétés esthétiques et supposées énergétiques.
example_3_translation: Rose quartz is prized by collectors for its aesthetic and purported energetic properties.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'KWARTS'. Stress on 'KWARTS'. One syllable, German loanword.
memory_hook: From German 'Quarz' derived from Slavic 'tvardy' (hard). A hard mineral.
related_words: minéral, cristal, silice, géologie, roche, électronique
common_mistakes: Quartz does not change form; 'quartz' is both singular and plural in French.

primary_meaning: recapitulation, a summary or restatement of main points previously discussed
secondary_meaning: rhetorical technique of briefly restating key arguments for emphasis and clarity
usage_notes: Use with 'une récapitulation', 'faire une récapitulation', 'récapitulation des points clés'
common_collocations: récapitulation des points clés, récapitulation finale, brève récapitulation, récapitulation logique
image_search_query: summary outline key points list educational presentation
image_context: Educational presentation board showing summarized key points and bullet-point recap
example_1: La récapitulation claire des points clés facilite grandement la compréhension de l'exposé complexe.
example_1_translation: A clear recapitulation of key points greatly facilitates understanding of the complex presentation.
example_2: Le professeur effectua une brève récapitulation des concepts étudiés avant l'examen final.
example_2_translation: The professor gave a brief recapitulation of the concepts studied before the final exam.
example_3: La structure rhétorique avec récapitulation aide les auditeurs à retenir l'information.
example_3_translation: The rhetorical structure with recapitulation helps listeners retain information.
related_words: résumé, synthèse, rhetorique, présentation, point clé, argumentation

primary_meaning: synchronous, occurring or existing at the same time; simultaneous
secondary_meaning: relating to communication or processing where events happen in real-time coordination
common_collocations: mode synchrone, communication synchrone, moteur synchrone, transmission synchrone
image_search_query: synchronized timing clock real-time digital communication
image_context: Computer screen showing synchronized clock signals and real-time data coordination
example_1: Les signaux synchrones permettent une coordination précise et fiable des systèmes informatiques.
example_1_translation: Synchronous signals allow precise and reliable coordination of computer systems.
example_2: La communication synchrone requiert la présence simultanée des interlocuteurs.
example_2_translation: Synchronous communication requires the simultaneous presence of interlocutors.
example_3: Les moteurs synchrones sont largement utilisés dans les applications industrielles modernes.
example_3_translation: Synchronous motors are widely used in modern industrial applications.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'sihn-KROHN'. Stress on 'KROHN'. The 'y' sounds like 'i'.
memory_hook: From Greek 'syn-' (together) + 'chronos' (time). Together in time; simultaneous.
related_words: synchronisation, simultané, coordination, temps réel, technologie, moteur
common_mistakes: Not confusing 'synchrone' (simultaneous) with 'diachronique' (across different time periods).

primary_meaning: tonic, relating to or producing muscular tension or strengthening effect
secondary_meaning: invigorating or strengthening in effect; pertaining to treatments that restore vigor
common_collocations: propriétés toniques, effet tonique, substance tonique, plante tonique
image_search_query: herbal tonic medicine strengthening wellness health drink
image_context: Traditional herbal tonic preparation with tonifying herbs and ingredients
example_1: Les propriétés toniques de certaines plantes ont été reconnues par la médecine traditionnelle depuis des siècles.
example_1_translation: The tonic properties of certain plants have been recognized by traditional medicine for centuries.
example_2: Une pause tonique avec une boisson appropriée pourrait améliorer la concentration mentale des élèves.
example_2_translation: A tonic break with an appropriate drink could improve students' mental concentration.
example_3: La médecine ayurvédique utilise de nombreuses herbes toniques pour restaurer l'équilibre du corps.
example_3_translation: Ayurvedic medicine uses many tonic herbs to restore bodily balance.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'toh-NIK'. Stress on 'NIK'. Similar to English 'tonic'.
memory_hook: From Greek 'tonos' (tone/tension). Something that gives tone or strengthens.
related_words: fortifiant, vigueur, santé, herbe médicinale, revitalisant, tonalité
common_mistakes: 'Tonique' (tonic) is not the same as 'tonalité' (tonality in music) or 'tonus' (muscle tone).

primary_meaning: ulna, the longer of the two bones of the forearm, located on the pinky side
secondary_meaning: the bone running from elbow to wrist on the medial side of the forearm
usage_notes: Use with 'l'ulna', 'l'ulna et le radius', 'fracture de l'ulna'
common_collocations: ulna et radius, fracture de l'ulna, olécrane, tête de l'ulna
image_search_query: ulna forearm bone anatomy skeleton anterior view
image_context: Anatomical diagram of human forearm showing ulna and radius bone structure
example_1: L'ulna est l'un des deux os majeurs de l'avant-bras, situé du côté du petit doigt.
example_1_translation: The ulna is one of the two major bones of the forearm, located on the pinky side.
example_2: Une fracture de l'ulna associée au radius peut nécessiter une immobilisation prolongée.
example_2_translation: A fracture of the ulna associated with the radius may require prolonged immobilization.
example_3: L'olécrane, apophyse de l'ulna, forme le point du coude.
example_3_translation: The olecranon, an ulnar process, forms the point of the elbow.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'UHL-nah'. Stress on 'UHL'. Latin anatomical term.
memory_hook: From Latin 'ulna'. The arm bone near the elbow. Named for its length or elbow prominence.
related_words: radius, avant-bras, olécrane, os, squelette, anatomie
common_mistakes: Not confusing 'ulna' (forearm bone) with 'cubitus' (elbow) or 'humérus' (upper arm bone).

primary_meaning: valve, a membranous structure that allows blood to flow in one direction only
secondary_meaning: one-way gate structure in the heart or blood vessels preventing backflow
usage_notes: Use with 'une valvule', 'les valvules cardiaques', 'insuffisance valvulaire'
common_collocations: valvule cardiaque, valvule aortique, valvule mitrale, insuffisance valvulaire
image_search_query: heart valve anatomy cardiac blood flow one-way
image_context: Cross-section diagram of heart showing four cardiac valves and blood flow direction
example_1: Les valvules cardiaques garantissent que le sang circule toujours dans le bon sens.
example_1_translation: Heart valves ensure that blood always flows in the correct direction.
example_2: Le dysfonctionnement des valvules peut entraîner de graves problèmes cardiovasculaires.
example_2_translation: Valve dysfunction can lead to serious cardiovascular problems.
example_3: La valvule mitrale est l'une des quatre valvules principales du cœur humain.
example_3_translation: The mitral valve is one of the four main valves of the human heart.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'val-VUL'. Stress on 'VUL'. The final 'e' is silent.
memory_hook: From Latin 'valva' (folding door). A structure that opens and closes to control flow.
related_words: cœur, santé, circulation sanguine, cardiologie, insuffisance, sténose
common_mistakes: 'Valvule' (small valve) vs 'valve' (larger valve) - both used in cardiology contexts.

primary_meaning: Williams syndrome, a rare genetic disorder caused by chromosomal deletion
secondary_meaning: neurodevelopmental condition characterized by cardiovascular problems and cognitive patterns
usage_notes: Use with 'le syndrome de Williams', 'patient Williams', 'génétique Williams'
common_collocations: syndrome de Williams, délétion 7q11.23, Williams-Beuren, hypercalcémie
image_search_query: chromosomal deletion genetic disorder Williams syndrome diagnosis
image_context: Karyotype showing chromosomal deletion characteristic of Williams syndrome
example_1: Le syndrome de Williams est causé par une microdélétion chromosomique spécifique.
example_1_translation: Williams syndrome is caused by a specific chromosomal microdeletion.
example_2: Les personnes atteintes du syndrome de Williams présentent souvent une hypersociabilité remarquable.
example_2_translation: People with Williams syndrome often present remarkable hypersociability.
example_3: Le diagnostic précoce du syndrome de Williams permet une prise en charge médicale optimale.
example_3_translation: Early diagnosis of Williams syndrome allows optimal medical management.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced 'WIL-yams'. English surname retained in French medical terminology.
memory_hook: Named after Dr. J.C.P. Williams who first described the syndrome in 1961.
related_words: génétique, syndrome, chromosome, délétion, maladie rare, diagnostic
common_mistakes: Williams syndrome includes both cardiovascular and developmental features - not just cognitive.
